title: President Trump's Coal Power Orders: A Legal Problem
summary: A long time ago, the United States used a lot of coal to make electricity. But coal plants are old and expensive, so many have been closing. President Trump wants to keep these coal plants open using a special rule from World War II. This rule was made for emergencies, like during a war, to help with electricity needs. Experts say using it now is wrong and against the law because it stops careful planning by utility companies. This could make electricity bills higher and hurt the environment. People are going to court to challenge these orders. The rule is being used to help the coal industry instead of for real emergencies.

background_read: Coal is a black rock that can be burned to make electricity. In the past, many countries used coal a lot, but it can pollute the air and cause health problems. Utility companies plan for years to make sure we have enough electricity without wasting money. Emergency rules are special laws that leaders can use quickly in big problems, like during a war or a natural disaster, to keep things running. When rules are used in the wrong way, it can cause confusion and harm people and the planet.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Trump and his Department of Energy
WHAT: Using an old emergency rule to keep coal plants open
WHY: To help the coal industry, but experts say it's illegal and bad for planning

perspectives.description: They believe the emergency orders are illegal because the rule is meant for short-term wartime needs, not long-term industry support.
